{"step_by_step_thinking": "Sweating is a direct method of heat loss from the body through evaporation of sweat from the skin. Vasodilation allows for increased blood flow to the skin, which enhances heat dissipation. Posture can influence heat loss by changing the surface area exposed to the environment, but it is not a direct physiological method of heat loss. Peripheral vasoconstriction reduces blood flow to the skin, decreasing heat loss. Therefore, posture is not a direct physiological method of heat loss.", "answer_choice": "C"}
